title: OSINT: AI-Powered Threat Intelligence

modules.title: What OSINT Is and Why AI Changed Everything
modules.sub: Understanding how AI transformed open-source intelligence gathering
modules.description: Learners explore the foundations of OSINT and examine how AI tools have dramatically accelerated an adversary's ability to collect, correlate, and weaponize publicly available data. They analyze real-world examples of AI-assisted reconnaissance to understand the scale and speed of modern threats. By the end, learners can articulate why traditional data exposure assumptions no longer hold in an AI-augmented threat landscape.

modules.title: Mapping Your Attack Surface with AI-Augmented Tools
modules.sub: Using AI-powered tools to inventory your organization's public footprint
modules.description: Learners are introduced to a curated set of AI-augmented OSINT tools and apply them to map the external-facing data their organization exposes across domains, infrastructure, and personnel. They practice interpreting tool outputs to identify which assets, credentials, or metadata create exploitable entry points. This module builds the technical foundation needed to conduct a full organizational OSINT audit.

modules.title: Social Engineering Fuel: How Adversaries Use Public Data
modules.sub: Tracing how exposed data powers phishing, fraud, and targeted attacks
modules.description: Learners step into the adversary's perspective and trace how publicly available employee information, organizational charts, and social media activity are assembled into convincing phishing and fraud campaigns. They evaluate specific data types — job postings, LinkedIn profiles, press releases — and assess the social engineering risk each one creates. Learners leave with a sharper instinct for which exposures translate directly into human-targeted attack vectors.

modules.title: Hands-On: Running OSINT Audits on Your Own Organization
modules.sub: Conducting a structured, repeatable OSINT audit from start to finish
modules.description: Learners follow a structured audit methodology to systematically investigate their own organization as an outside threat actor would, documenting findings across people, technology, and data categories. They prioritize discovered exposures by risk severity and begin drafting remediation recommendations tied to specific findings. This module produces a tangible audit artifact learners can bring directly back to their security team.

modules.title: Defensive OSINT: Monitoring, Alerts, and Data Minimization
modules.sub: Building ongoing workflows to detect and reduce public data exposure
modules.description: Learners design a continuous monitoring workflow using alerting tools and data minimization strategies to ensure sensitive information is detected quickly when it surfaces publicly. They evaluate options for reducing organizational data exposure at the source — from tightening metadata on published documents to enforcing stricter social media policies. The module closes with learners producing a prioritized action plan that combines immediate fixes with a sustainable long-term monitoring posture.

synopsis: Attackers now use AI to harvest and correlate public data at machine speed — building convincing fraud campaigns and targeted intrusions from information your organization freely exposes. This course teaches security professionals to think like adversaries, run structured OSINT audits, and close the gaps before threat actors exploit them.
rationale: AI has collapsed the time and skill barrier for adversarial reconnaissance, making OSINT-driven fraud and social engineering a top-tier risk that most security curricula still treat as a footnote.
learning_outcome: Learners can conduct a structured AI-augmented OSINT audit of their organization, identify high-risk data exposures, and implement a monitoring workflow to detect when sensitive information surfaces publicly.
source_signals: Launch HN: Overwatch (YC S22): OSINT platform for cyber and fraud risk
